rounds.name: The WiFi Sonar
rounds.premise: Turn invisible ambient WiFi waves into a zero-camera spatial radar that sees through solid walls.
rounds.hook: Right now thousands of invisible radio waves are passing through your chest and bouncing your heartbeat back to your router.
rounds.motif: Concentric radio wave ripple overlays illuminating physical room contours in darkness
rounds.why_it_fits: Grounds the complex physics of Channel State Information wave distortion in a vivid perceptual metaphor.

rounds.name: The Anti Camera
rounds.premise: Monitor your health and home security in total darkness without generating or storing a single pixel of video.
rounds.hook: What if you could track your sleep, detect falls, and guard your house without placing a single creepy camera indoors?
rounds.motif: Camera lenses shattering into abstract wireframe skeletal poses derived purely from RF data
sections: Setting Up Home Assistant Automation Triggers
rounds.why_it_fits: Directly targets the core value proposition of non-optical, privacy-first spatial intelligence.

rounds.name: The Eight Dollar Miracle
rounds.premise: Squeeze a 55 kilobyte Rust AI model onto an eight dollar ESP32 chip to turn radio noise into spatial intelligence.
rounds.hook: This eight dollar piece of silicon is running a spatial AI model inside fifty-five kilobytes of memory.
rounds.motif: ESP32 pinout diagrams paired with real-time memory byte allocation counters
rounds.why_it_fits: Focuses on the extraordinary engineering accomplishment of running DensePose WiFi tracking on ultra-cheap hardware.
